channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model enabling efficient on-device function calling for mobile applications. This allows complex tasks to be executed offline, enhancing user experience with low latency.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools, allowing developers to create more capable AI agents. This ecosystem supports connections to platforms like GitHub and Notion.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring an engaging save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to energize participants ahead of the keynotes.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, focusing on advancements in agentic AI and specialized technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building and practical applications.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views, enhancing collaboration between developers and AI.

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
item.description: Claude Code subscribers will face additional fees for using OpenClaw and other third-party tools, increasing the cost of accessing Anthropic's coding assistant.

item.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
item.description: Folk musician Murphy Campbell found unauthorized versions of her songs on Spotify, leading her to suspect AI-generated fakes. This incident highlights the challenges of copyright in the digital age.

item.title: Wearables Are Getting Very Messy
item.description: Wearable technology company Whoop is facing a lawsuit, indicating ongoing legal challenges in the wearables market.

item.title: videosdk-plugins-openai 1.0.3
item.description: VideoSDK released version 1.0.3 of its plugin for OpenAI services, enhancing integration capabilities for developers. This update signifies ongoing advancements in AI application development.

item.title: videosdk-plugins-langchain 1.0.3
item.description: VideoSDK launched version 1.0.3 of its plugin for LangChain and LangGraph, allowing easier AI workflows. This reflects the growing trend of AI tools integration.

item.title: videosdk-plugins-anthropic 1.0.3
item.description: VideoSDK introduced version 1.0.3 of its plugin for Anthropic, expanding its plugin ecosystem. This move highlights the increasing collaboration in AI technologies.

item.title: The CMS is dead. Long live the CMS
item.description: A veteran WordPress agency announced a shift from WordPress to AI, signaling a potential decline in traditional CMS usage. This reflects a broader trend of embracing AI in web development.

item.title: lmux-azure-foundry 0.4.1
item.description: Version 0.4.1 of lmux's Azure AI Foundry provider was released, enhancing cloud AI capabilities. This update signifies important developments in cloud-based AI solutions.

item.title: lmux-anthropic 0.3.1
item.description: lmux released version 0.3.1 of its Anthropic provider, improving AI service integration. This reflects ongoing enhancements in AI service offerings.

item.title: linekeeper 1.0.2
item.description: Linekeeper version 1.0.2 was released to help preserve line numbers in AI interactions, aiding developers in maintaining code clarity. This tool is useful for enhancing productivity.

item.title: Modder uses Claude AI to rewrite BIOS so they can boot unsupported 12 P-core Bartlett Lake CPU in Windows on a Z790 motherboard
item.description: A modder successfully used Claude AI to rewrite BIOS, enabling unsupported CPUs to boot on specific motherboards. This showcases AI's potential in hardware modifications.

item.title: AI recruiting startup Mercor hit by cyberattack; Meta halts collaboration
item.description: AI recruiting startup Mercor was hit by a cyberattack linked to LiteLLM, raising concerns about security in AI tools. This incident underscores the vulnerabilities in widely used AI systems.

item.title: pymimir-rgnn 0.2.6
item.description: Version 0.2.6 of pymimir-rgnn was released, providing a new Relational Graph Neural Network package for Mimir based on PyTorch. This update adds valuable tools for AI researchers.

item.title: torchtext2 added to PyPI
item.description: torchtext2 has been added to PyPI, enhancing the PyTorch ecosystem with new functionalities for text processing. This addition signifies ongoing improvements in AI tools for developers.

item.title: pytorch-bsf 0.18.0
item.description: The release of pytorch-bsf 0.18.0 introduces a PyTorch implementation of Bézier simplex fitting, providing developers with new tools for optimization tasks. This is significant for those working on geometric modeling and optimization in AI.

item.title: thermoclaw 0.1.6
item.description: Thermoclaw 0.1.6 offers diagnostics for PyTorch optimizers to catch training failures early, potentially saving GPU resources. This tool is crucial for improving training efficiency in AI projects.

item.title: lmux-openai 0.4.0
item.description: lmux-openai 0.4.0 provides an OpenAI provider for the lmux framework, facilitating integration of OpenAI's capabilities into applications. This is significant for developers looking to leverage OpenAI's tools easily.

item.title: Do less with AI
item.description: The article discusses the importance of working smarter with AI rather than just relying on automation, emphasizing strategic use of technology. This perspective is vital for maximizing AI's potential in various industries.

item.title: lmux-azure-foundry 0.4.0
item.description: lmux-azure-foundry 0.4.0 introduces an Azure AI Foundry provider for the lmux framework, expanding its capabilities for developers using Azure services. This enhances the integration of Azure's AI tools.

item.title: Apple: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation
item.description: Apple's research reveals that large language models can enhance code generation through simple self-distillation, without needing external verification. This finding could lead to more efficient model training practices.

item.title: open-agent-traces 0.1.0
item.description: open-agent-traces 0.1.0 is a mock OCEL 2.0 event log generator designed for LangChain multi-agent runs, aiding developers in testing and debugging. This tool is useful for improving multi-agent system workflows.

item.title: open-agent-traces added to PyPI
item.description: open-agent-traces has been added to PyPI, providing a mock OCEL 2.0 event log generator for LangChain multi-agent runs. This addition supports developers in enhancing their multi-agent applications.

item.title: langchain-pythia 0.1.1
item.description: langchain-pythia 0.1.1 integrates LangChain with Pythia Oracle, enabling on-chain calculation of crypto indicators via Chainlink. This integration is significant for developers in the blockchain and crypto space.

item.title: Weekend reads: Half of social science ‘doesn’t replicate’; ‘Scientific ghosts: Life after retraction’; multisensory learning paper retracted
item.description: Retraction Watch highlights significant issues in social science research, including a new database of retracted papers and the challenges of replicating studies. This week’s focus emphasizes the ongoing concerns about research integrity.

item.title: Centre funds 12 AI projects for sovereign models; BharatGen gets 4x the next highest allocation at over Rs 1000 crore
item.description: The Indian government has allocated over Rs 1000 crore to 12 AI projects under the IndiaAI Mission, with BharatGen receiving the largest share. This initiative aims to develop sovereign AI models to enhance the country's technological capabilities.

item.title: The cofounder shakeup at xAI is vintage Elon Musk
item.description: xAI has experienced a leadership shakeup with the departure of its last non-Musk cofounder, raising questions about the startup's future and its upcoming IPO plans. This change reflects Elon Musk's strong influence over the company.

item.title: Apple’s Biggest Shift Ever? iPhone Fold Rumored to Debut Alongside iPhone 18 Pro this September
item.description: Apple is rumored to launch its first foldable iPhone, the 'iPhone Fold,' alongside the iPhone 18 Pro in September 2026, marking a significant milestone for the company. This release coincides with Apple's 50th anniversary.

item.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
item.description: As generative AI technology advances, concerns grow about the authenticity of creative works, with calls for clearer labeling of AI-generated content. This reflects a broader skepticism about the role of AI in creative industries.

item.title: Inside the German state trying to break free from Microsoft
item.description: Schleswig-Holstein is undertaking a significant tech overhaul to reduce reliance on Microsoft, representing a broader trend in Europe to challenge US tech dominance. This initiative could serve as a model for other regions.

item.title: ETtech Deals Digest: Startups raise $187 million this week, down 28% on-year
item.description: Startups raised $187 million this week, a 28% decline compared to the same period last year, with fewer deals taking place. This downturn reflects a challenging funding environment for emerging companies.

item.title: AI is rewiring Bollywood, the world's most prolific film industry
item.description: AI is reshaping Bollywood by improving filmmaking processes and creating new content that aligns with audience preferences. This transformation highlights the industry's adaptation to technological advancements.

item.title: Samsung S90H OLED TV: Everything you need to know
item.description: Samsung has launched the S90H OLED TV, positioned just below its flagship model for 2026. The S90H offers significant features and improvements, catering to high-end consumers.

item.title: What Is the Jevons Paradox and What Does It Mean for Micron and Sandisk Investors After Google's Revolutionary AI Breakthrough?
item.description: The Jevons Paradox suggests that as technology improves efficiency, overall consumption may increase, impacting investors in companies like Micron and Sandisk following Google's AI advancements. Understanding this paradox is crucial for strategic investment decisions.

item.title: LFM2.5-350M: No Size Left Behind | Liquid AI
item.description: Liquid AI has launched LFM2.5-350M, an enhanced model with extensive pre-training and improved inference speed, aimed at broad applications. This upgrade signifies a notable advancement in AI model capabilities.

item.title: chandan-cortex added to PyPI
item.description: The chandan-cortex has been added to PyPI, offering a personal AI assistant that is simple and powerful while prioritizing local-first functionality. This addition could enhance accessibility to AI tools for developers.

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: Anthropic is currently a hot commodity in the private markets, with increased trading activity, while OpenAI is losing traction. The potential IPO of SpaceX could significantly alter the investment landscape.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Sharing recap access
item.description: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing recording and transcript owners to grant access to specific individuals, streamlining the sharing process. This update is expected to enhance collaboration efficiency.

item.title: Chuck Norris' Family Warns of AI-Generated Videos Spreading 'Fabricated Reports' About His Health Before Death
item.description: Chuck Norris' family has issued a warning about AI-generated videos spreading false health reports about him, highlighting concerns over misinformation in the age of AI. This incident underscores the potential dangers of deepfake technology.

item.title: Developer behind controversial AI apps sues Apple over App Store takedowns
item.description: The developer behind controversial AI apps is suing Apple for what it claims are arbitrary removals from the App Store, raising questions about platform governance and developer rights. This lawsuit could set precedents for app distribution policies.

item.title: bernstein 1.4.13
item.description: The release of bernstein 1.4.13 focuses on declarative agent orchestration designed for engineering teams, aiming to streamline workflows and improve project management. This tool could enhance productivity in tech development.

item.title: azure-ai-evaluation 1.16.4
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Evaluation Library for Python has been updated to version 1.16.4, providing developers with enhanced tools for evaluating AI models. This update supports better model assessment practices.

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: Meta has paused its collaboration with Mercor following a data breach that may have compromised sensitive AI training data. This incident raises concerns about data security in the AI industry.

item.title: Charles Schwab Is Gearing Up to Offer Bitcoin, Ethereum Spot Trading
item.description: Charles Schwab plans to offer spot trading for Bitcoin and Ethereum by the end of the quarter, marking a significant move into cryptocurrency trading for the financial giant. This could attract more traditional investors to the crypto market.
